events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need parse scenario: residential job, overcurrent device selection & coordination, observed field condition: missing AFCI/GFCI where required. Work rough-complete. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. Missing AFCI/GFCI where required is non-compliant and safety risk (shock/fire). Risk high or critical? Missing AFCI/GFCI can cause shock/fire; in residential required by NEC. Likely high. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 80? rough-complete. value? Could be number of missing devices? Not given. Could set 0 or null? Schema says value: 0 default. Since no numeric reading, value 0. sound_source etc default. order empty. workable empty. findings: AFCI/GFCI protection missing on required branch circuits. actions: install listed AFCI/GFCI breakers/outlets per NEC. rationale: NEC 210.8 GFCI, 210.12 AFCI requirements; missing protection violates code and creates shock/fire hazard. references: NEC 210.8, 210.12, 240 maybe overcurrent protection, 110.14(C). Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text: {"decision":{"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":80,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Required AFCI/GFCI overcurrent protection is missing on branch circuits where the NEC mandates it","Omission creates shock and/or fire hazards for occupants and violates prescriptive code requirements"],"actions":["Identify all branch circuits requiring AFCI/GFCI protection per NEC 210.8 and 210.12","Install listed AFCI/GFCI circuit breakers or receptacles as appropriate","Verify proper termination torque and conductor temperature ratings per NEC 110.14(C)","Re-inspect before closing out the rough-in"],"rationale":"At the rough-complete stage, branch-circuit overcurrent devices must include required AFCI and GFCI protection. Their absence means the installation is non-compliant and unsafe, so the work fails inspection.","references":["NEC 210.8","NEC 210.12","NEC 240","NEC 110.14(C)"]}}
